description_long: This object is a slender, lightweight airplane with an exceptionally long, narrow wingspan and a minimalistic fuselage. The wings are straight and slightly dihedral, with multiple small struts or supports visible beneath them, suggesting a design optimized for gliding or solar-powered flight. The tail section features a T-tail configuration, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a vertical fin. The landing gear consists of several small, fixed struts distributed along the wings and fuselage, indicating a lightweight, possibly unmanned or experimental aircraft. The surface appears smooth and dark, possibly covered with solar panels or a composite material. There is no visible cockpit, suggesting remote or autonomous operation.
description: A slender, long-winged airplane with a T-tail, fixed strut landing gear, and a smooth, dark surface, likely designed for lightweight or solar-powered flight. No cockpit is visible, indicating remote or autonomous operation.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a front oblique angle, highlighting the long, narrow wings and the T-tail at the rear.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the slight dihedral angle of the wings and the minimalistic, straight fuselage.
description_view_2: The rear oblique angle emphasizes the T-tail and the distribution of landing gear struts beneath the wings.
description_view_3: A direct front view showcases the full wingspan, the thin fuselage, and the symmetrical placement of landing gear struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and multiple underwing struts; no winglets or sweep.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with multiple small struts distributed along the wings and fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No cockpit visible; appears to be unmanned or remotely operated.
